task_completion.description: Verify the creation of the 'Sprint 4 - Payment Overhaul' version with correct dates.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the version 'Sprint 4 - Payment Overhaul' in the project settings/versions list or sprint backlog, showing a start date of 2025-07-01 and a due date of 2025-07-31.

task_completion.description: Verify that both specified work packages are assigned to the new sprint.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for 'Fix broken checkout on mobile Safari' and 'Add wishlist feature' listed under the 'Sprint 4 - Payment Overhaul' version in the work packages list, taskboard, or sprint backlog.

task_completion.description: Verify the status of 'Fix broken checkout on mobile Safari' is updated to 'In progress'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the work package details or list view for 'Fix broken checkout on mobile Safari' and check that its status badge clearly shows 'In progress'.

task_completion.description: Verify that 2 hours of time were logged on 'Fix broken checkout on mobile Safari' with the correct comment.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the 'Time and costs' or 'Logged time' section of the 'Fix broken checkout on mobile Safari' work package. It should show 2.0 hours logged with the comment 'Sprint planning and initial investigation'.

task_completion.description: Verify the creation of the 'Sprint 4 Release Notes' wiki page with the required content.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a wiki page titled 'Sprint 4 Release Notes' in the project. The content should mention the sprint name, both work packages ('Fix broken checkout on mobile Safari', 'Add wishlist feature'), and a summary of payment overhaul goals.

integrity.description: The agent used the OpenProject web interface to perform the tasks.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the OpenProject web UI in a browser, not terminal commands, API scripts (like curl or python requests), or direct database manipulation.

integrity.description: The agent actually navigated through the OpenProject menus and forms.
integrity.visual_evidence: Presence of intermediate screens like form filling for versions, work package editing, time logging modals, and wiki editors, rather than just a final text dump.

integrity.description: The agent did not use browser developer tools to fake the UI state.
integrity.visual_evidence: The browser developer console/inspector should not be open and actively modifying DOM elements to fake the presence of the sprint, time logs, or wiki page.
